background: If cat ownership and dog ownership were independent, then \operatorname{Pr}(A$ and $B)$ would equal \operatorname{Pr}(A) \times \operatorname{Pr}(B)=0.35 \times 0.30=0.105.

task.answer: Therefore, these events are not independent.
task.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

meta info.caption: Independence (Pet ownership example).
meta info.section: 5.5  More RULES AND PROPERTIES OF PROBABILITY
